title: TC001-Login with valid credentials redirects to Dashboard
description: Verifies that an existing user can sign in with email/password and is redirected to the protected dashboard.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Navigate to the login page using the explicit path /login (http://localhost:3000/login).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Fill the email field (index 1861) with [ENV_TEST_LOGIN_EMAIL], fill the password field (index 1862) with [ENV_TEST_LOGIN_PASSWORD], then click the Login button (index 1863).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/label[1]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('[ENV_TEST_LOGIN_EMAIL]')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/label[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('[ENV_TEST_LOGIN_PASSWORD]')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/login' in frame.url
        assert '/dashboard' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Dashboard').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login attempt failed - 'Invalid login credentials' message displayed on the login page.
- After submitting credentials the application did not navigate to the dashboard; current URL remains '/login'.
- Protected dashboard content ('Dashboard') is not visible on the page.
- No successful authentication observed with the provided credentials; the user remains on the login form.

title: TC002-Login with invalid credentials shows authentication error
description: Verifies that wrong email/password prevents login and shows a visible authentication error message.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Giriş Yap' link (element index 140) to open the login page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[1]/nav/div/div[2]/a[1]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login using the navigate action (explicit step provided).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type '[ENV_TEST_ALT_LOGIN_EMAIL]' into the email field (index 1995), then type '[ENV_TEST_ALT_LOGIN_PASSWORD]' into the password field (index 1996), then click the 'Giriş Yap' button (index 1997).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/label[1]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('[ENV_TEST_ALT_LOGIN_EMAIL]')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/label[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('[ENV_TEST_ALT_LOGIN_PASSWORD]')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert "/login" in frame.url
        raise AssertionError("Element with text 'Invalid' not found in the available elements; cannot assert authentication error visibility. Task done.")
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC007-After login, user can access a protected route via in-app navigation
description: Verifies an authenticated session allows navigation to a protected page without being sent back to login.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Giriş Yap' (Login) link to open the login page (use element index 146).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[1]/nav/div/div[2]/a[1]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Navigate to /login using the explicit navigation step (http://localhost:3000/login).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Type '[ENV_TEST_LOGIN_EMAIL]' into the email field (index 2122).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/label[1]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('[ENV_TEST_LOGIN_EMAIL]')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/label[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('[ENV_TEST_LOGIN_PASSWORD]')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Attempt submitting the login form again by clicking the 'Giriş Yap' button once more, then check for redirect to /dashboard.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Type '[ENV_TEST_LOGIN_EMAIL]' into the email field (index 2259) and submit the login form (then check for redirect to /dashboard).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/label[1]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('[ENV_TEST_LOGIN_EMAIL]')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/label[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('[ENV_TEST_LOGIN_PASSWORD]')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Fill the email (2416) and password (2420) fields with test credentials and click the Login button (2424) to attempt authentication; then verify redirect to /dashboard.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/label[1]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('[ENV_TEST_LOGIN_EMAIL]')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/label[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('[ENV_TEST_LOGIN_PASSWORD]')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/dashboard' in frame.url
        assert '/assets' in frame.url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- ASSERTION: Login failed - 'Invalid login credentials' error displayed after submitting the provided test credentials.
- ASSERTION: Authenticated session not established - the application did not redirect to /dashboard; current URL remains /login.
- ASSERTION: Protected page access (/assets) could not be verified because authentication failed and navigation to the protected area could not be performed.

title: TC010-Create a maintenance rule for an asset and verify it appears in the list
description: Verifies a user can add a maintenance rule for a selected asset and sees it listed with a next due date.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000/
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/", wait_until="commit", timeout=10000)
        
        # -> Click the 'Giriş Yap' link to open the login page.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[1]/nav/div/div[2]/a[1]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Ensure the login page loads. Use the visible 'Giriş Yap' link (index 146) to navigate to the login page by clicking it again (preferred over direct navigation). If the page still does not change after this click, report failure.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[1]/nav/div/div[2]/a[1]').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # -> Fill the email field with [ENV_TEST_LOGIN_EMAIL], fill the password field with [ENV_TEST_LOGIN_PASSWORD], then click the 'Giriş Yap' (Sign in) button.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/label[1]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('[ENV_TEST_LOGIN_EMAIL]')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/label[2]/input').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('[ENV_TEST_LOGIN_PASSWORD]')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/dashboard' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Add maintenance rule').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login failed - 'Invalid login credentials' message is displayed on the login page.
- Dashboard page did not load after authentication attempt; current URL remains /login.
- Maintenance functionality could not be tested because the user is not authenticated and the dashboard was not reachable.

        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.fill('[ENV_TEST_LOGIN_PASSWORD]')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/main/div[2]/section[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000); await elem.click(timeout=5000)
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert '/dashboard' in frame.url
        await expect(frame.locator('text=No data').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Add your first asset').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await expect(frame.locator('text=Log a service').first).to_be_visible(timeout=3000)
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
